result.stdout:
You are using me wrongly!
Usage: Ecore2Code [Options] -o [Output File or directory] [Inputfiles]
Input files may either be in NMeta or Ecore format.
Example: Ecore2Code -f -n NMF.Models -o Meta NMeta.nmf
NMF Ecore2Code 2.0.462.1+fa6fc2d5f408ef9d89dd739e785685303396ae47
Copyright © Georg Hinkel 2013

  -n, --namespace             The root namespace

  -l, --language              (Default: CS) The language in which the code
                              should be generated

  -f, --folder                Determines whether the code for classes should be
                              separated to multiple files

  -p, --parallel              If specified, runs the code generator in parallel
                              mode (in incubation)

  -x, --force                 If specified, the code is generated regardless of
                              existing code

  -u, --model-uri             If specified, overrides the uri of the base
                              package.

  -p, --primitive-types       If set, Ecore Data types are transformed to
                              primitive types

  -o, --output                Required. The output file/folder in which the code
                              should be generated

  -m, --metamodel             Specify this argument if you want to serialize the
                              NMeta metamodel possibly generated from Ecore

  --save-nmeta                If set, Ecore2Code will save any metamodel
                              consumed in a different format that NMeta as an
                              NMeta file in the same directory.

  -r, --resolve               A list of namespace remappings with optional code
                              base namespace override in the syntax
                              URI@baseNamespace=file, multiple entries separated
                              by ';'

  -t, --type-mapping          A list of type mappings in the syntax <Ecore
                              Instance Class>=<.NET class>, multiple entries
                              separated by ';'

  -i, --input-only            If set, generate code for input files only

  --no-events                 If set, no dedicated events are generated for
                              properties. This has the same effect as no-changed
                              and no-changing together

  --no-changed                If set, no Changed events are generated for events

  --no-changing               If set, no Changed events are generated for events

  --collectionsAreElements    If set, collections are all rendered as elements

  --help                      Display this help screen.

  --version                   Display version information.

  value pos. 0
